name: Fried Alligator
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut fresh alligator tail into pieces no larger than 2" x 1 inch thick.
2. Place the pieces in a shallow dish. Add vinegar, salt, pepper, and cayenne pepper to taste. Let marinate for approximately 30 minutes.
3. While the gator is soaking, mix four parts corn meal with one part flour in a dusting bag or container.
4. Heat oil in a skillet to 400 degrees F (204 C).
5. Coat each piece of alligator in the corn meal and flour mixture.
6. Carefully place the coated pieces into the hot oil and fry until golden brown.
7. Serve immediately.
Suggestions: Try deep-fried Alligator nuggets as an appetizer.
